The genus Brodiaea is also known as the "California brodiaea" or "tall brodiaea". The classification of Brodiaea sessilis is based on its physical characteristics and genetic makeup.

intro: Brodiaea sessilis, also known as Western Brodiaea or Yellow Mariposa Lily, is a perennial plant species native to the western coast of North America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, and research has identified its health benefits, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities, which can aid in reducing pain, inflammation, and improving wound healing. The bioactive constituents of Brodiaea sessilis include alkaloids, flavonoids, and glycosides, such as seslin and brodialine, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. The plant is commonly used to make herbal preparations, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are used to treat various health conditions and promote overall well-being.
